student_name: Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari
hub_subtitle: Inglês do dia a dia — apresentar-se, rotina e conversação

lesson.menu_desc: Talking about the future in everyday life -- making predictions, promises, and quick decisions with will -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 11 -- Predictions & Promises
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 11 -- Predictions & Promises
lesson.grammar_point: will for predictions, promises, and spontaneous decisions

listenings.text: People often ask me what I think the future will look like, and honestly, I love making predictions. I am certain that technology will change the way we work, and I expect that many of us will work from home much more. I doubt that big offices will completely disappear, but I think they will become smaller. The weather is harder to predict; even the best forecast is sometimes wrong. On a personal level, I promise myself one thing every January: I will learn something new. This year, it is a language. I am not sure I will speak it perfectly, but I am certain I will try my best. Nobody knows exactly what will happen next, and maybe that is the best part of the future.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I just had a spontaneous idea and I had to call you. The weather forecast for Sunday looks perfect, so I wonder if you want to go to the beach with me. I promise it will be a relaxing day, with no work and no phones, just the sea. I doubt it will be crowded, because it is still early in the season. If you are tired, do not worry; I will drive, so you can just relax. I expect we will be back home by six. Oh, and I will bring lunch for both of us. Call me back when you can and tell me if you are free. I really think you will love it. Talk soon, bye!

extra_audio.text: First, start with what you notice or with the weather forecast. Then make your prediction using will. After that, say how certain you are, with 'I am certain' or 'I doubt'. Next, offer some help or make a promise with will. Finally, end with a friendly warning or a kind word.
